retry_reason: Put operation unconditionally decrements; if the object was already freed, calling put again is a use-after-free bug. Retry is not meaningful.
contract_summary: cached_dev_put is a put wrapper that decrements the refcount of a cached_dev object. When the refcount reaches zero, it schedules the detach work to asynchronously clean up the object. The caller must ensure that each reference acquired with a previous get is paired with exactly one put. After calling cached_dev_put, the caller must not access the dc object again, as it may be freed after the work completes. This put is unconditional; there is no return value to check.

judge.reason: The only purpose is to decrement the refcount and conditionally schedule detach work; no other business logic.

check.reason: The refcount_dec_and_test call operates on &dc->count, which is dc.count.refs. The access path and type chain are consistent: parameter dc is struct cached_dev, field count is refcount_t, and refcount_t contains refs of type atomic_t. The segment counts match (3 each), direction put is correct for a dec-and-test operation.
